content:
# Exodus Chapter 35 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 106–107; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 78–79 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_035_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_035_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_035_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_035_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE WILLING HEART:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 35 across pages 106–107, immediately following the radiant-face narrative of chapter 34. This chapter opens with a renewed Sabbath command, then pivots to an extraordinary moment in the Torah: YHWH's dwelling is funded entirely by freewill offering. The key phrase nesiv libo ("his heart lifted him") recurs as the engine of generosity — the tabernacle is built not by taxation or conscription but by hearts stirred and spirits made willing. The Vaticanus LXX (pp. 78–79) substantially rearranges material in this section, with significant expansions and reorderings of the tabernacle inventory compared to MT.

---

## The Sabbath Command Renewed (vv. 1–3)

**1** And Moses assembled [va-yaqhel] all the congregation [kol-adat] of the Sons of Israel, and he said to them, "These are the words [ha-devarim] which YHWH has commanded [tsivvah] to do [la'asot] them:

> **Key terms:** וַיַּקְהֵל (va-yaqhel) = "and he assembled/gathered" — Hiphil of קהל; this verb gives the parashah its name (Vayaqhel). **LXX:** συνήθροισεν ("he gathered together"), matching MT.

**2** "Six days [sheshet yamim] work [melakhah] shall be done [te'aseh], and on the seventh day [u-va-yom ha-shevi'i] there shall be to you a holy thing [qodesh], a sabbath of complete rest [shabbat shabbaton] to YHWH. Everyone doing [kol-ha-oseh] work [melakhah] on it shall be put to death [yumat]."

> **Key terms:** שַׁבַּת שַׁבָּתוֹן (shabbat shabbaton) = "a sabbath of complete cessation" — the doubling intensifies: not merely rest but absolute stopping. יוּמָת (yumat) = "he shall be put to death" — Hophal of מות; the passive emphasizes communal enforcement. **LXX:** σάββατα ἀνάπαυσις ἁγία ("sabbaths, a holy rest"), softening the doubling. LXX retains θανάτῳ θανατούσθω ("by death let him be put to death"), preserving the emphasis.

**3** "You shall not kindle [lo teva'aru] a fire [esh] in any of your dwelling-places [be-khol moshevoteikhem] on the day of the Sabbath [be-yom ha-shabbat]."

> **Key terms:** לֹא תְבַעֲרוּ (lo teva'aru) = "you shall not kindle/burn" — Piel of בער; the only specific Sabbath prohibition named here. **LXX:** οὐ καύσετε πῦρ ("you shall not kindle fire"), matching MT precisely.

---

## The Call for Freewill Offerings (vv. 4–9)

**4** And Moses spoke to all the congregation of the Sons of Israel, saying, "This is the word [ha-davar] which YHWH has commanded [tsivvah], saying:

> **Key terms:** לֵאמֹר (lemor) = "saying" — the standard speech-introduction formula, doubled here (Moses saying what YHWH said saying). **LXX:** matches MT.

**5** "Take [qekhu] from among you [me-ittekhem] a contribution [terumah] for YHWH. Everyone whose heart is willing [kol nediv libbo] — let him bring it [yevi'eha], the contribution of YHWH [et terumat YHWH]: gold [zahav] and silver [va-khesef] and bronze [u-nekhoshet],

> **Key terms:** נְדִיב לִבּוֹ (nediv libbo) = "willing/generous of his heart" — from נדב ("to be willing, volunteer"); the heart is the seat of volition. תְּרוּמָה (terumah) = "contribution/raised-offering" — from רום ("to be high"); what is lifted up and set apart. **LXX:** κατὰ τὸ δυνατόν ("according to ability") is absent — Vaticanus follows MT with παρ᾽ ἑαυτῶν ("from yourselves"). ἀφαίρεμα ("contribution") for terumah.

**6** "and blue [u-tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet of worm [ve-tola'at shani], and fine linen [ve-shesh], and goat-hair [ve-izzim],

> **Key terms:** תְּכֵלֶת (tekhelet) = "blue-violet" — dye from the murex snail. תּוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי (tola'at shani) = "worm of scarlet" — crimson dye from the coccus ilicis insect. **LXX:** ὑάκινθον καὶ πορφύραν ("hyacinth and purple"), standard equivalents.

**7** "and skins of rams [ve-orot eilim] made-red [me'oddamim], and skins of takhash [ve-orot tekhashim], and acacia wood [va-atsei shittim],

> **Key terms:** תְּחָשִׁים (tekhashim) = meaning uncertain — possibly dolphin-skin, dugong, or fine leather; left untranslated. מְאָדָּמִים (me'oddamim) = "reddened" — Pual participle of אדם. **LXX:** δέρματα κριῶν ἠρυθροδανωμένα ("skins of rams dyed red") and δέρματα ὑακίνθινα ("hyacinth-colored skins") — LXX renders tekhashim as a color rather than an animal.

**8** "and oil [va-shemen] for the light [la-ma'or], and spices [u-vesamim] for the oil of anointing [le-shemen ha-mishkhah] and for the incense of fragrances [ve-liqtoret ha-sammim],

> **Key terms:** קְטֹרֶת הַסַּמִּים (qetoret ha-sammim) = "incense of the fragrances/spices" — the compound incense formula of 30:34–38. **LXX:** τὴν σύνθεσιν τοῦ θυμιάματος ("the composition of the incense").

**9** "and onyx stones [ve-avnei shoham], and stones of settings [ve-avnei millu'im] for the ephod [la-efod] and for the breastpiece [ve-la-khoshen]."

> **Key terms:** אַבְנֵי שֹׁהַם (avnei shoham) = "onyx/lapis stones" — identification debated. אַבְנֵי מִלֻּאִים (avnei millu'im) = "stones of filling/setting" — gems set into the metalwork. **LXX:** λίθους σαρδίου ("sardius stones") for shoham — a different identification; λίθους εἰς τὴν πλήρωσιν ("stones for the filling"), matching millu'im.

---

## The Inventory of Items to Make (vv. 10–19)

**10** "And every wise-hearted one [ve-khol khakham-lev] among you shall come and make [ve-ya'asu] all that [et kol-asher] YHWH has commanded [tsivvah]:

> **Key terms:** חֲכַם־לֵב (khakham-lev) = "wise of heart" — wisdom in the Hebrew Bible is practical skill, not abstract intellect; the craftsman's wisdom. **LXX:** σοφὸς τῇ καρδίᾳ ("wise in heart"), matching MT.

**11** "the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], its tent [et-oholo] and its covering [ve-et-mikhsehu], its clasps [et-qerasav] and its boards [ve-et-qerasav], its bars [et-berikhav], its pillars [et-ammudav], and its bases [ve-et-adanav];

> **Key terms:** מִשְׁכָּן (mishkan) = "dwelling-place" — from שׁכן ("to dwell"); YHWH's earthly residence. **LXX:** τὴν σκηνήν ("the tent"), standard rendering.

**12** "the ark [et-ha-aron] and its poles [ve-et-baddav], the mercy-seat [et-ha-kapporet], and the veil [ve-et parokhet] of the screen [ha-masakh];

> **Key terms:** כַּפֹּרֶת (kapporet) = "mercy-seat/atonement-cover" — from כפר ("to cover/atone"); the gold lid where atonement is enacted. פָּרֹכֶת (parokhet) = "veil/curtain" — the partition separating the Holy of Holies. **LXX:** τὸ ἱλαστήριον ("the propitiatory/place of mercy") for kapporet; τὸ καταπέτασμα ("the curtain") for parokhet.

**13** "the table [et-ha-shulkhan] and its poles [ve-et-baddav] and all its vessels [ve-et-kol-kelav], and the bread of the Presence [ve-et lekhem ha-panim];

> **Key terms:** לֶחֶם הַפָּנִים (lekhem ha-panim) = "bread of the face/presence" — literally "face-bread"; bread set before YHWH's face continually. **LXX:** τοὺς ἄρτους τῆς προθέσεως ("the loaves of the setting-forth"), an interpretive rendering.

**14** "and the lampstand of the light [ve-et-menorat ha-ma'or] and its vessels [ve-et-keleha], and its lamps [ve-et-neroteiha], and the oil of the light [ve-et shemen ha-ma'or];

> **Key terms:** מְנֹרַת הַמָּאוֹר (menorat ha-ma'or) = "lampstand of the luminary/light" — the seven-branched candelabrum. **LXX:** τὴν λυχνίαν τοῦ φωτός ("the lampstand of the light"), matching MT.

**15** "and the altar of incense [ve-et-mizbach ha-qetoret] and its poles [ve-et-baddav], and the oil of anointing [ve-et shemen ha-mishkhah], and the incense of fragrances [ve-et qetoret ha-sammim], and the screen of the entrance [u-masakh ha-petakh] for the entrance of the tabernacle [le-fetakh ha-mishkan];

> **Key terms:** מָסָךְ הַפֶּתַח (masakh ha-petakh) = "screen of the entrance" — the outer hanging, distinct from the inner parokhet. **LXX:** τὸ κάλυμμα τῆς θύρας τῆς σκηνῆς ("the covering of the door of the tent").

**16** "the altar of the burnt-offering [et mizbach ha-olah] and the bronze grating [ve-et-mikhbar ha-nekhoshet] which belongs to it, its poles [et-baddav] and all its vessels [ve-et-kol-kelav], the basin [et-ha-kiyyor] and its stand [ve-et-kanno];

> **Key terms:** מִכְבַּר הַנְּחֹשֶׁת (mikhbar ha-nekhoshet) = "bronze grating/network" — from כבר ("to interweave"). כִּיּוֹר (kiyyor) = "basin/laver" — for priestly washing. **LXX:** τὸν λουτῆρα ("the laver") for kiyyor.

**17** "the hangings of the court [et qal'ei he-khatser], its pillars [et-ammudav] and its bases [ve-et-adaneiha], and the screen of the gate of the court [ve-et masakh sha'ar he-khatser];

> **Key terms:** קַלְעֵי הֶחָצֵר (qal'ei he-khatser) = "hangings/curtains of the court" — from קלע ("to sling"); fabric screens. **LXX:** τὰ ἱστία τῆς αὐλῆς ("the sails/curtains of the court").

**18** "the pegs of the tabernacle [et-yitdot ha-mishkan] and the pegs of the court [ve-et-yitdot he-khatser] and their cords [ve-et-meitareihem];

> **Key terms:** יְתֵדֹת (yitdot) = "pegs/stakes" — from יתד; the anchoring system. מֵיתָרֵיהֶם (meitareihem) = "their cords/ropes." **LXX:** τοὺς πασσάλους ("the stakes") and τὰ σχοινία ("the ropes").

**19** "the woven garments [et-bigdei ha-serad] for ministering [le-sharet] in the holy place [ba-qodesh], the holy garments [et-bigdei ha-qodesh] for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons [ve-et-bigdei vanav] for serving as priests [le-khahen]."

> **Key terms:** בִּגְדֵי הַשְּׂרָד (bigdei ha-serad) = "garments of the service/woven-work" — serad is a rare term, possibly meaning "plaited/woven." **LXX:** τὰς στολὰς τὰς λειτουργικάς ("the liturgical robes"), an interpretive expansion. Note: LXX Vaticanus significantly rearranges the tabernacle inventory in this section, grouping items differently from MT.

---

## The People's Willing Response (vv. 20–29)

**20** And all the congregation of the Sons of Israel went out [va-yetse'u] from before [mi-lifnei] Moses.

> **Key terms:** וַיֵּצְאוּ (va-yetse'u) = "and they went out" — they depart to gather their offerings. **LXX:** ἐξῆλθον ("they went out"), matching MT.

**21** And they came [va-yavo'u] — every man whose heart lifted him [kol-ish asher nesa'o libbo] and everyone whose spirit made him willing [ve-khol asher nadevah rukho oto] — they brought [hevi'u] the contribution of YHWH [et-terumat YHWH] for the work of the tent of meeting [li-mlekhet ohel mo'ed] and for all its service [u-le-khol avodato] and for the holy garments [u-le-vigdei ha-qodesh].

> **Key terms:** נְשָׂאוֹ לִבּוֹ (nesa'o libbo) = "his heart lifted him" — the heart is the active agent, carrying the person toward generosity. נָדְבָה רוּחוֹ (nadevah rukho) = "his spirit made him willing" — from נדב; the spirit volunteers of its own impulse. These two phrases together form the theological engine of the chapter: divine gift meets human willingness. **LXX:** ᾧ ἔδοξεν τῇ ψυχῇ ("to whom it seemed good to the soul") — flattens the two distinct Hebrew expressions into one.

**22** And they came, the men with [al] the women — every willing-hearted one [kol nediv lev] — they brought brooches [hevi'u khakh] and earrings [va-nezem] and rings [ve-tabba'at] and ornaments [ve-khumaz], every article of gold [kol-keli zahav]; and every man who waved [henif] a wave-offering [tenufah] of gold to YHWH —

> **Key terms:** חָח (khakh) = "brooch/clasp" — a decorative pin. כוּמָז (khumaz) = "ornament" — a rare word, possibly a pendant or armlet. תְּנוּפָה (tenufah) = "wave-offering" — from נוף ("to wave"); the ritual gesture of presentation. **LXX:** lists the jewelry items similarly; renders tenufah as ἀφαίρεμα ("contribution"), losing the wave-gesture nuance.

**23** And every man with whom [asher nimtsa itto] was found blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet of worm [ve-tola'at shani] and fine linen [ve-shesh] and goat-hair [ve-izzim] and skins of rams made-red [ve-orot eilim me'oddamim] and skins of takhash [ve-orot tekhashim] — they brought them.

> **Key terms:** אֲשֶׁר נִמְצָא אִתּוֹ (asher nimtsa itto) = "with whom was found" — Niphal of מצא; the materials are simply "found" with them, as though providentially placed. **LXX:** παρ᾽ οἷς εὑρέθη ("with whom was found"), matching MT.

**24** Every one lifting [kol merim] a contribution [terumat] of silver [kesef] and bronze [u-nekhoshet] brought [hevi'u] the contribution of YHWH [et terumat YHWH]; and everyone with whom [ve-khol asher] was found [nimtsa itto] acacia wood [atsei shittim] for any work of the service [le-khol melekhet ha-avodah] — they brought it.

> **Key terms:** מֵרִים (merim) = "one lifting/raising" — Hiphil participle of רום; the same root as terumah; the person who "lifts up" brings the "lifted-offering." **LXX:** matches MT in content.

**25** And every woman wise of heart [ve-khol-ishah khakhmat-lev] spun [tavu] with her hands [be-yadeha], and they brought [va-yavi'u] the spun-yarn [matve]: the blue [et-ha-tekhelet] and the purple [ve-et-ha-argaman], the scarlet of worm [et-tola'at ha-shani] and the fine linen [ve-et-ha-shesh].

> **Key terms:** חַכְמַת־לֵב (khakhmat-lev) = "wise of heart" — feminine construct; women are explicitly named as possessors of craft-wisdom. מַטְוֶה (matve) = "spun-yarn/spinning" — from טוה ("to spin"). **LXX:** ἔνησαν ταῖς χερσίν ("they spun with their hands"), matching MT.

**26** And all the women whose heart lifted them [asher nasa libban] in wisdom [be-khokmah] spun [tavu] the goat-hair [et-ha-izzim].

> **Key terms:** נָשָׂא לִבָּן (nasa libban) = "their heart lifted them" — the same phrase from v. 21 now applied to women; the heart-lifting is linked explicitly to wisdom (be-khokmah). **LXX:** αἷς ἔδοξεν τῇ διανοίᾳ ("to whom it seemed good in understanding") — substitutes "understanding" for the heart-lifting.

**27** And the leaders [ve-ha-nesi'im] brought the onyx stones [et avnei ha-shoham] and the stones of settings [ve-et avnei ha-millu'im] for the ephod [la-efod] and for the breastpiece [ve-la-khoshen],

> **Key terms:** הַנְּשִׂאִם (ha-nesi'im) = "the leaders/chieftains" — from נשׂא ("to lift/bear"); the tribal heads contribute the precious stones. Note: the Leningrad Codex spells this defectively (without yod), which later midrash interprets as criticism of the leaders for contributing last. **LXX:** οἱ ἄρχοντες ("the rulers").

**28** and the spice [ve-et-ha-bosem] and the oil [ve-et-ha-shamen] for the light [la-ma'or] and for the oil of anointing [u-le-shemen ha-mishkhah] and for the incense of fragrances [ve-liqtoret ha-sammim].

> **Key terms:** בֹּשֶׂם (bosem) = "spice/balsam" — aromatic resin. **LXX:** τὰς συνθέσεις ("the compounds"), slightly interpretive.

**29** Every man and woman whose heart made them willing [asher nadev libbam otam] to bring [le-havi] for all the work [le-khol-ha-melakhah] which YHWH had commanded [tsivvah] to do [la'asot] by the hand of Moses — the Sons of Israel brought [hevi'u] a freewill-offering [nedavah] to YHWH.

> **Key terms:** נְדָבָה (nedavah) = "freewill-offering/voluntary gift" — from נדב; the chapter's closing summary term for the entire collection. נָדַב לִבָּם (nadev libbam) = "their heart made willing" — the verb and noun share the same root; what the heart volunteers becomes the freewill-offering. **LXX:** ἑκούσιον ("willing/voluntary"), a fair equivalent.

---

## Bezalel and Oholiab Appointed (vv. 30–35)

**30** And Moses said to the Sons of Israel, "See [re'u], YHWH has called [qara] by name [be-shem] Bezalel [Betsal'el] son of Uri [ben-Uri] son of Hur [ben-Khur] of the tribe of Judah [le-matteh Yehudah].

> **Key terms:** בְּצַלְאֵל (Betsal'el) = "in the shadow of God" — the master craftsman's very name declares divine protection/proximity. קָרָא בְשֵׁם (qara be-shem) = "called by name" — personal, individual election. **LXX:** κέκληκεν... ἐξ ὀνόματος ("he has called... by name"), matching MT.

**31** "And he has filled him [va-yemalei oto] with the spirit of God [ruakh Elohim], in wisdom [be-khokmah], in understanding [bi-tvunah], and in knowledge [u-ve-da'at], and in every craft [u-ve-khol-melakhah],

> **Key terms:** רוּחַ אֱלֹהִים (ruakh Elohim) = "spirit of God" — the same phrase from Genesis 1:2; the spirit that hovered over creation now fills the craftsman. The three-fold wisdom sequence: חָכְמָה (khokmah, "wisdom"), תְּבוּנָה (tvunah, "understanding/discernment"), דַּעַת (da'at, "knowledge") — comprehensive creative capacity. **LXX:** πνεῦμα θεῖον σοφίας καὶ συνέσεως ("divine spirit of wisdom and understanding"), compressing the list.

**32** "and to devise designs [ve-lakhshov makhashavot], to work [la'asot] in gold [ba-zahav] and in silver [u-va-kesef] and in bronze [u-va-nekhoshet],

> **Key terms:** לַחְשֹׁב מַחֲשָׁבֹת (lakhshov makhashavot) = "to think/devise thoughts/designs" — cognate accusative; the craftsman's imagination mirrors divine creativity. **LXX:** ἀρχιτεκτονεῖν ("to be a master-builder"), a significant interpretive shift toward Greek architectural vocabulary.

**33** "and in cutting of stone [u-ve-kharoshet even] for setting [le-malle], and in cutting of wood [u-ve-kharoshet ets], to do [la'asot] every craft of design [be-khol melekhet makhashevet].

> **Key terms:** חֲרֹשֶׁת (kharoshet) = "cutting/carving" — skilled engraving work. לְמַלֵּא (le-malle) = "to fill/set" — the same root as millu'im ("settings"); gems are "filled" into their mountings. **LXX:** κατεργάζεσθαι τοὺς λίθους ("to work the stones").

**34** "And to teach [u-le-horot] he has put [natan] in his heart [be-libbo] — he and Oholiab [hu ve-Oholi'av] son of Ahisamach [ben-Akhisamakh] of the tribe of Dan [le-matteh-Dan].

> **Key terms:** וּלְהוֹרֹת (u-le-horot) = "and to instruct/teach" — Hiphil infinitive of ירה; the same root as Torah; Bezalel is not only a craftsman but a teacher. אָהֳלִיאָב (Oholi'av) = "my father's tent" — his name evokes the very tent he is building. **LXX:** καὶ προβιβάσαι ("and to instruct"), matching MT.

**35** "He has filled them [mille otam] with wisdom of heart [khokhmat-lev] to do [la'asot] every work [kol-melekhet] of an engraver [kharash] and a designer [ve-khoshev] and an embroiderer [ve-roqem] in the blue [ba-tekhelet] and in the purple [u-va-argaman], in the scarlet of worm [be-tola'at ha-shani] and in the fine linen [u-va-shesh], and a weaver [ve-oreg] — doers [osei] of every craft [kol-melakhah] and devisers of designs [ve-khoshevei makhashavot]."

> **Key terms:** חָרָשׁ (kharash) = "engraver/craftsman" — one who incises. חֹשֵׁב (khoshev) = "designer/thinker" — from חשׁב; the one who conceives patterns. רֹקֵם (roqem) = "embroiderer" — one who works colored thread into fabric. אֹרֵג (oreg) = "weaver" — the basic textile craftsman. This verse catalogues the full spectrum of artisanal skill, all sourced from divine filling (mille). **LXX:** distinguishes ποικιλτής ("variegator") and ὑφάντης ("weaver"), mapping onto the Hebrew categories.

---

## Manuscript Notes

**Leningrad Codex (pp. 106–107):** The text flows continuously from the end of chapter 34 (Moses' radiant face) into 35 with a petuhah (open paragraph) break. The defective spelling of ha-nesi'im in v. 27 (without yod) is clearly visible in the manuscript and is a well-known masoretic feature. Parashah Vayaqhel begins at v. 1. The masorah marginalia are present on both pages.

**Codex Vaticanus (pp. 78–79):** The LXX version of Exodus 35–40 is notably different from MT in arrangement. Vaticanus places much of the tabernacle construction material in a different sequence, with chapters 35–40 substantially rearranged compared to the Hebrew. For this chapter specifically, the freewill-offering section and the appointment of Bezalel track MT reasonably closely, but the detailed tabernacle inventory (vv. 11–19) shows variant ordering. The LXX consistently renders the "heart-lifting" vocabulary with milder Greek expressions, losing some of the Hebrew's visceral quality.
